theme: Plant a few zealots to flip a crowd
title: Ridgeline Valley: Planting the New Rootstock
seed_problem: minimum target-set selection for majority-threshold (bootstrap-percolation-style) dynamics on a graph
mutation: composite: (1) replace the uniform-cost exact minimum target set with a NON-UNIFORM recruitment cost per node (a knapsack-flavored budget dimension) and (2) replace 'convert the whole graph to a fixed point' with 'reach a POPULATION-WEIGHTED coverage threshold tau within a FIXED R-round horizon', which removes the classic fixed-point/monotone-closure endpoint and makes both WHICH nodes and HOW MANY within a time budget matter
innovation_hook: Majority-dynamics tipping is achieved by nucleating self-sustaining local-majority domains, favoring dense pockets over global hubs.
strategies: trivial: recruit every household as a seed grower (reproduces the checker's do-everything baseline, always feasible, ratio ~0.1)

naive greedy: rank households once by raw weighted trust-degree (the 'best-connected household is most influential' recipe), binary-search the smallest degree-ranked prefix that clears the coverage target -- ignores cost and is drawn to cheap-looking high-degree hubs whose ties carry little leverage over any single neighbor

insight (strong): rank households once by a leverage score -- for each tie, the SQUARE of the tie weight divided by the neighbor's own total trust-weight, cost-normalized -- which rewards nodes whose neighbors are themselves low-degree (dense pocket members, where one tie is a big share of a neighbor's vote) over hub nodes (whose many neighbors are each barely moved by one hub tie), then binary-searches the smallest leverage-ranked prefix that clears the target; this nucleates whole pockets into self-sustaining NEW domains instead of chasing raw connectivity
